personality_one_liner: An elegant, warm, and content-led culinary journal where food photography and typography take center stage.

spacing.rule: Use magazine-like spacing rhythms with generous vertical breathing room. Let typography and whitespace carry the hierarchy. Elements should feel grouped logically, not cramped.

do's: Use frosted glass over food imagery on featured/hero cards to preserve readability.
Apply to sticky top headers when content scrolls underneath to maintain editorial elegance.
Use on the bottom tab bar to create seamless scrolling depth.
dont's: Never use glass on plain form inputs, buttons, or standard list items.
Avoid heavy blurs that completely obscure the warmth of the background images.

screens.name: Home
screens.purpose: A visually inspiring feed and categorized collection of the user's saved recipes.

states.loading: Centered minimalist spinner with warm text 'Warming the ovens...'
states.empty: Illustration of an empty plate and wooden table, message: 'Your recipe book is empty', CTA: 'Add First Recipe'
states.error: Readable text 'We couldn't fetch your recipes.' + Retry button.

screens.layout:
1. Sticky Top Search Bar: prominent search input filtering by title/ingredient. 
2. Category Filter Chips: Horizontally scrolling pills directly beneath the search. 
3. Featured Recipe Hero: Full-width card with image, 3-stop gradient scrim, and large typography overlay. 
4. Recipe List Grid: 2-column grid structure utilizing 'flexWrap: wrap' with generous gap. Each item shows a 1:1 image, title in serif, and compact metadata (time, difficulty).

screens.name: Recipe Detail
screens.purpose: An immersive reading experience guiding the cook flawlessly from ingredients to execution.

states.error: Readable text 'Failed to load this recipe.' + Retry button.

screens.layout:
1. Hero Image: Edge-to-edge top cover spanning safe area, with a strong vertical gradient scrim (top-to-bottom fade) to ensure text contrast. 
2. Title & Metadata: Large Fraunces title layered onto the bottom of the image, with a glass FAB overlay for 'Favorite'. 
3. Content Sections: Distinct, generously spaced editorial blocks for Ingredients (bulleted) and Instructions (numbered, larger line-height). 
4. Persistent Actions: Keep edit/share capabilities in the header or as floating actions.

screens.name: Add/Edit Recipe
screens.purpose: A seamless, distraction-free data entry form.

states.error: Standard form validation error messages in muted rust (#B84A43).

screens.layout:
1. Image Picker Header: A prominent placeholder block (surfaceSecondary) to tap and upload a photo. 
2. Form Fields: Single-column flow with labels strictly above inputs. 
3. Category Select: Chip-based selection instead of a hidden dropdown. 
4. Dynamic Lists: Addable text inputs for ingredients and instructions. 
5. Sticky CTA: 'Save Recipe' pinned above the keyboard in a fixed bottom container.

lunch_salad.alt: Fresh salad bowl with grilled chicken

dinner_spaghetti.alt: Gourmet spaghetti on a white plate

snack_trail_mix.alt: Healthy trail mix bowl on wooden table

empty_state.alt: Elegant empty plate on a table

instructions_to_main_agent: Adopt the 'Editorial Mobile LIGHT' personality. Focus on magazine-style spacing (use xl/2xl tokens), high-contrast readability, and letting food photography dominate the visual hierarchy.
Typography: Use Fraunces (serif) purely for headings/recipe titles and DM Sans for body text/ingredients. Do not exceed font weight 500.
Zero tolerance for blue, indigo, or purple. The app's palette relies entirely on warm tones (terracotta, sage, cream, oat).
Whenever overlaying text on a food image (like the detail screen hero or featured cards), you MUST apply the 3-stop vertical gradient scrim (transparent to >70% surfaceInverse) behind the text.
Form constraints: Use single-column layouts for the Add/Edit form, labels above inputs, and a sticky 'Save' button fixed above the keyboard inside a KeyboardAvoidingView.
Seed the app's local storage with the provided food photos (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Dessert/Snack) to ensure the UI is rich immediately upon load.
